user_intent: Review the site and improve its seo ability google bussiness requires a sitemap be generated what else is missing?
assistant_text:
I'll review the site structure first to understand what's there, then assess SEO gaps and generate a sitemap.

I now have the full picture. This is a 2-page static site (`index.html`, `resources.html`) served by a Bun server, domain `wellnessbeyondlljc.org`. Here's what's missing for SEO, in priority order — I'll implement all of it now since these are safe, additive files.

**Gaps found:**
1. ❌ **sitemap.xml** (Google requires this)
2. ❌ **robots.txt** (should point to sitemap)
3. ❌ **Canonical URLs** (prevents duplicate-content issues)
4. ❌ **Open Graph + Twitter Card meta** (no link previews when shared on Facebook/Instagram/TikTok — which this org actively uses)
5. ❌ **JSON-LD structured data** — biggest win for a nonprofit: an `NGO`/`Organization` schema with `sameAs` social links is what feeds Google's Knowledge Panel and ties to Google Business
6. ❌ **site.webmanifest + favicon basics + theme-color**

Let me build them.

Now let me grab the real theme background color from the CSS so my `theme-color` matches the site.

Dark theme bg is `#0c0a09`. Let me fix the manifest to match.

Now the head meta for `index.html` — canonical, Open Graph, Twitter Card, theme-color, manifest, and the nonprofit JSON-LD:

Now the same head additions for `resources.html` (canonical points to its own URL, og:type article-ish but website is fine):

Now let me verify everything serves correctly by starting the dev server and checking the new files plus content types.

`site.webmanifest` returned no content-type — Bun doesn't recognize that extension. Let me check and fix the server to set it (otherwise browsers may reject the manifest).

Browsers expect `application/manifest+json` for the manifest. Let me add a small content-type override in the server for that extension.

All done and verified on the dev server. Here's what I added.

## Implemented

**New files**
- **`sitemap.xml`** — both pages (`/` and `/resources.html`) with lastmod/priority. ✅ serves as `application/xml`
- **`robots.txt`** — allows all crawlers + points to the sitemap. ✅
- **`site.webmanifest`** — PWA/icon metadata, theme color matched to the site's `#0c0a09`. ✅ now serves as `application/manifest+json`

**`index.html` + `resources.html` `<head>`**
- `<link rel="canonical">` (self-referencing per page)
- `<meta name="robots" content="index, follow">`
- `<meta name="theme-color">`
- **Open Graph** tags (title, description, url, image, site_name) — so Facebook/Instagram/TikTok shares show a real preview card
- **Twitter Card** tags (`summary_large_image`)
- `apple-touch-icon` + `manifest` links

**`index.html` — JSON-LD structured data** (the biggest SEO win for a nonprofit): a schema.org `NGO`/`Organization` block with name, logo, description, email, and `sameAs` linking the three social profiles. This is what Google uses to build a Knowledge Panel and connect the site to your Google Business listing.

**`server.ts`** — added `application/manifest+json` content-type for `.webmanifest` (Bun didn't infer it).

## Next steps for you (can't be done from code)
1. **Submit the sitemap** in [Google Search Console](https://search.google.com/search-console) → verify domain ownership, then add `https://wellnessbeyondlljc.org/sitemap.xml`. This is the step that satisfies the Google requirement.
2. **Google Business Profile** — claim/verify it and set the website to `wellnessbeyondlljc.org` so it links to the JSON-LD `sameAs` graph.

## Recommended follow-ups (optional, not done)
- **Proper social share image**: I'm reusing `logo.png` (1260×1440 portrait). A dedicated **1200×630** landscape image gives much better link previews. Want me to note this as a task?
- **Custom `404.html`** — the server currently returns plain-text 404.
- **`EIN` / postal address** in the JSON-LD if this is a registered 501(c)(3) — strengthens the Organization schema and donor trust.

One caveat: the `.webmanifest` content-type fix only affects the Bun dev server. On wherever the site is actually hosted in production, confirm the host serves that file as `application/manifest+json` too.

Want me to add the 1200×630 OG image and a `404.html`?

user_intent: Yes but no ein yet to share.
assistant_text:
Let me check what image tooling is available to produce a proper 1200×630 PNG.

ImageMagick is available. Let me grab the brand colors and an available serif font, then compose the card.

ImageMagick available with DejaVu-Serif-Bold (closest to the site's serif). Brand: bg `#0c0a09`, gold `#d6a24a`, cream `#f4ede0`. Let me compose the 1200×630 card.

The logo already contains the "WELLNESS BEYOND #LLJC" wordmark, so my right-side title is redundant and clipping. Let me redesign — bigger logo, and the right side just carries the tagline + URL.

That looks clean and professional. Let me save it into the project and wire it up.

Now update the OG/Twitter image references in both pages to the new card (with width/height):

Now the **404 page**. I'll create a styled `404.html` matching the site, then wire the server to serve it with a real 404 status.

Now verify the full set end-to-end:

Everything verified working. Both requested items are done — no EIN added to the schema (left out until you have it).

## Added this round

**1200×630 social share image** — `assets/img/og-image.png`
- Brand-matched dark card: full logo lockup on the left, gold divider, the "Building Resilience / Teaching Compassion / Empowering Youth" tagline and URL on the right.
- Wired into both pages as `og:image` + `twitter:image`, with `og:image:width`/`height` (1200/630) so Facebook, Instagram, TikTok, LinkedIn, and iMessage render a proper large preview card instead of a cropped portrait logo.

**Custom `404.html`**
- Full site chrome (header, footer, theme toggle), an "Error 404" message, **Return home** / **Find resources** buttons, and the 988 crisis banner so a lost visitor in distress still sees help.
- Marked `noindex, follow` so Google won't index the error page.
- `server.ts` now serves it with a real **HTTP 404** status (verified) — important so search engines treat missing URLs as gone, not as soft-200 duplicates.

## Verified
- `og-image.png`, `/`, `/resources.html`, `/sitemap.xml`, `/robots.txt`, `/site.webmanifest` → all **200**
- Unknown URL → **404** and renders the styled page ✓

## When you have the EIN
Tell me the number and I'll add it to the JSON-LD as:
```json
"taxID": "XX-XXXXXXX",
"nonprofitStatus": "Nonprofit501c3"
```
That, plus a verified Google Business Profile, is what unlocks the donor-trust signals and Knowledge Panel.

One reminder from before: the only thing that satisfies Google's "needs a sitemap" requirement on their end is **submitting `https://wellnessbeyondlljc.org/sitemap.xml` in Google Search Console** after verifying domain ownership — the file is now in place and ready for that.

user_intent: use the hero image for social needs
assistant_text:
The hero image is `assets/img/logo.png` (the full logo lockup, 1260×1440). I'll rebuild the social card to be that hero logo on the brand background at the required 1200×630 — so it uses the hero image but still meets social dimensions without cropping or transparency issues.

Clean, centered, on-brand. Let me replace the OG image with this version.

Done. The social/OG image is now the **hero logo on the brand background**, sized to 1200×630 with no cropping or transparency.

Since the meta tags already point to `assets/img/og-image.png`, replacing the file in place is all that was needed — no HTML changes. Both `index.html` and `resources.html` `og:image`/`twitter:image` references are still valid (verified serving at 200 earlier).

So the final social setup:
- **Image**: hero logo lockup, centered on `#0c0a09` with the gold border, 1200×630
- **Used by**: Facebook/Open Graph + Twitter/X cards on both pages
- **Dimensions declared** via `og:image:width`/`height`

When you share `wellnessbeyondlljc.org` on Facebook, Instagram, TikTok, or iMessage, you'll get this card. Note: those platforms cache previews aggressively — after the site is live, run the URL through Facebook's [Sharing Debugger](https://developers.facebook.com/tools/debug/) and click "Scrape Again" to force a fresh fetch.
